title: Allow one local user to control two players for dev testing
description: Add a development/testing-only option that lets a single local user drive two player seats in the browser. This should make it easier to exercise turn flow, move selection, and multi-player interactions without coordinating multiple humans. Keep the behavior clearly scoped to local/dev fixtures or lobby options so normal game rules and production multiplayer assumptions are not changed.
close_reason: Implemented dev shared local control lobby option

title: Epic: Implement Sword gameplay logic
description:
Track the remaining Sword/Swords gameplay work as separate implementation tickets. Current state: resolve-sword-command validates browser-free Sword commands in src/main/gnostica/game_state/sword.cljc, while browser move selection still returns :move-transition-unavailable for Sword powers.

Split work:
- Pure piece attack transition.
- Pure territory attack transition.
- Major Sword variant and sequencing edge cases.
- Browser Sword staging and confirmation wiring after pure contracts settle.
acceptance_criteria:
- Child tickets exist for the pure piece attack, pure territory attack, Sword major/variant edge cases, and browser staging work.
- The epic remains open until those tickets are implemented, tested, and the docs/mind map are reconciled.
close_reason: All child Sword implementation tickets are closed; current docs and code track remaining browser major work under bd-ydt.

title: Implement Sword piece attack transition
description:
Implement pure Sword attacks against piece targets on top of the existing resolve-sword-command validation.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add or extend the public apply-sword-move facade so resolved :piece targets mutate pure game state through structured success/error maps.
- Shrink large pieces to medium and medium pieces to small; destroy small pieces.
- Return removed/replaced pieces to the correct owner stash and keep player stash mirrors plus [:pieces :stashes] reconciled.
- Preserve enemy survivor orientation; allow only resolver-validated current-player survivor orientation.
- Discard hand-source Sword cards exactly once and append useful Sword history events.
- Add focused unit and/or feature coverage for success paths, over-damage/rejection paths already normalized by the resolver, and Malli schema/stash accounting invariants.
close_reason: Implemented pure Sword piece attack transition

title: Implement Sword territory attack transition
description:
Implement pure Sword attacks against territory targets on top of the existing resolve-sword-command validation.

Acceptance criteria:
- Extend apply-sword-move so resolved :territory targets mutate pure game state through structured success/error maps.
- Reduce territory card value by selected damage for non-destroying attacks using a valid replacement card.
- Destroy territories when damage equals the territory value, moving the destroyed card to discard and preserving valid surrounding board/piece invariants.
- Require replacement cards for non-destroying attacks; reject missing, reused, or invalid replacements without mutating state.
- Support Tower discard-pile replacement rules while keeping normal Sword replacement sources limited to hand cards.
- Preserve legal pieces and orientations on surviving territory coordinates.
- Add focused unit and/or feature coverage for success paths, replacement rejection paths, destruction, and deck/discard/schema accounting.
close_reason: done

title: Cover Sword major variants and sequencing edge cases
description:
Close the rule gaps around major-arcana Sword variants after the base piece and territory attack transitions exist.

Acceptance criteria:
- Verify Justice, Death, and Moon plain Sword powers use the same attack contract where appropriate.
- Verify Tower uses the discard-pile replacement option only where resolve-sword-command allows it.
- Verify Magician wild-suit Sword behavior is carried through from gnostica.cards/sword-variants into attack application.
- Add focused tests for variant identity in history/events and for source-card discard behavior.
- Document any deliberately deferred multi-step or browser-staged major behavior in README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md if implementation is intentionally partial.
close_reason: done

title: Wire browser Sword staging and confirmation
description:
Implement browser move-selection support for Sword attacks after the pure apply-sword-move contract is available.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add staged Sword controls for target kind, target piece/territory, damage, replacement source/card, and current-player survivor orientation where applicable.
- Build app-state confirmation commands that call apply-sword-move and update :game only on success.
- Preserve structured gameplay errors on rejected confirmations without clearing the staged selection.
- Remove the Sword-specific :move-transition-unavailable path only for the implemented Sword confirmation flow.
- Keep Sun unavailable behavior unchanged.
- Add app-state/move-selection tests and browser smoke coverage where practical for the Sword panel flow.
close_reason: done

title: Clarify release build prerequisites for server and smoke docs
description: README.md and AGENTS.md list clojure -M:server and describe clojure -M:smoke as serving the released app, but they do not make the prerequisite explicit: a current browser build must already exist, normally from clojure -M:release, unless SMOKE_URL targets an existing dev or release server. Tighten the command docs so stale or missing src/main/resources/js/main.js is an obvious failure mode.
close_reason: Documented release build prerequisite for server and smoke commands

title: Antialias 3D board rendering
description:
Goal: make the Three.js board renderer produce visibly antialiased card, wasteland, and Icehouse piece edges.

Requirements:
- Enable antialiasing in the WebGL renderer where supported without breaking the existing r128 Three.js global runtime.
- Preserve the CSS fallback path for unavailable or incompatible WebGL runtimes.
- Verify canvas sizing and nonblank rendering still pass on desktop and mobile.
- Keep browser-free board layout math unchanged unless antialiasing exposes a tested geometry issue.

Acceptance criteria:
- The 3D board canvas uses antialiasing where the browser/GPU supports it.
- Card and piece outlines look smoother without changing selection behavior or layout.
- clojure -M:release and clojure -M:smoke pass.
close_reason: Implemented antialiased renderer setup with smoke coverage

title: Split the Three.js board renderer out of gnostica.app
description:
Problem: `src/main/gnostica/app.cljs` mixes re-frame events/subscriptions, UI components, Three.js runtime detection, board layout constants, texture loading, mesh construction, pointer raycasting, camera controls, and disposal in one namespace. The renderer is working, but the current shape makes it harder to review lifecycle safety, test pure layout decisions, and extend gameplay UI without touching WebGL internals.

Evidence:
- `three-board-scene` and helpers occupy most of `app.cljs`, from Three runtime helpers through component lifecycle and fallback status UI.
- Pure layout rules such as card positions, piece slots, and selection mapping are embedded in browser/Three-specific code.

Acceptance criteria:
- Move Three.js-specific scene creation, renderer lifecycle helpers, and board/piece layout constants into a dedicated renderer namespace.
- Keep `gnostica.app` focused on app state, subscriptions, and UI composition.
- Extract any pure layout/mapping helpers that can be tested without a browser or WebGL context.
- Add focused tests for extracted pure helpers where practical, especially card positioning, board index mapping, and piece slot limits.
- MIND_MAP.md and extern notes are updated if namespace ownership or Three.js API usage changes.
close_reason: Split renderer into gnostica.three-board, extracted browser-free board-layout helpers, added layout tests, and updated README/AGENTS/MIND_MAP docs.

title: No-piece turns can still expose draw when hand has room
description: The local rules say that if a player has no pieces on the board, they may only place a small piece on an empty territory or wasteland. The move-selection source availability only disables activate/play/orient when there are no owned pieces. Draw cards is enabled whenever max-draw-count is positive, so a no-piece player with fewer than six cards could draw instead of placing the initial small. This is masked at fresh setup because hands start full, but it becomes a real state-machine bug once piece destruction or other card movement creates a no-piece, under-full hand. Evidence: docs/rules.txt lines 47 to 55, README.md line 55, AGENTS.md line 39, and src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc source-unavailable-reason and max-draw-count. Enforce the no-piece restriction in source options and confirmation, then add an app-state test for a no-piece player with hand room.
close_reason: Fixed no-piece draw availability and confirmation guard

title: Draw faint dotted outlines around wasteland squares
description:
Problem: Wasteland spaces are rule-relevant empty squares adjacent to territories, but the board UI currently emphasizes only occupied territory cards. Players need a subtle visual cue for legal wasteland spaces without making the board feel cluttered.

Acceptance criteria:
- Identify board squares that are wastelands: empty spaces adjacent to at least one territory and not void spaces.
- Draw a faint dotted outline around each wasteland square in the primary Three.js board view.
- Add a matching faint dotted outline in the CSS fallback board view.
- Keep outlines behind cards and pieces so they do not interfere with selection, raycasts, piece placement, card readability, or future move-target highlighting.
- Make the outline visible enough on desktop and mobile but visually secondary to territory cards, selected cards, and pieces.
- Add focused browser-free layout/state tests if wasteland detection is extracted, and update smoke coverage or manual notes if renderer behavior changes.
- Update README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md if new board-layout helpers, renderer behavior, or verification guidance are added.
close_reason: Implemented browser-free wasteland detection, 3D dashed outlines, CSS fallback dotted outlines, docs, release build, and smoke verification.

title: Align README startup description with lobby-first browser flow
description:
Fresh-eye docs review found README has stale pre-lobby board-first wording. README.md:25 says the first screen renders the nine-card board, and README.md:29 says local browser startup opts into demo board pieces. Current app code and other docs say normal gnostica.fixtures/browser-init-options starts in a two-player lobby with no game/demo pieces; only smoke options bypass the lobby with :demo-board-pieces, and app-state/initialize applies demo pieces only when explicit options provide them. README.md:3 also still calls move selection early even though browser staging now covers nearly all implemented powers.

Acceptance: revise the README overview and 3D board section to say the normal first screen is the local lobby, the board appears after start or fixture bypass, demo pieces are explicit fixture/smoke data, and move-selection maturity wording matches AGENTS.md and MIND_MAP.md.
close_reason: Aligned README overview and 3D board docs with lobby-first startup, explicit fixture bypass, and current move-selection coverage.

title: Strength and Star Disc major behaviors are not stageable in the move panel

The browser move-selection layer only builds a single disc-command, exposes target orientation for grown current-player pieces, and does not provide controls for a second Strength action or Star's minion-orientation step. Star discard-pile replacement is exposed, but the orient-before-targeting part is not. Evidence: README.md line 51, AGENTS.md line 31, MIND_MAP.md node [46], src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc disc-requirements, disc-command, move-disc-orientation-available?, and app-state tests that cover Star replacement but not minion orientation or Strength double-action UI. Either add browser staging for these major-specific Disc flows or mark them as pure-only/unavailable so the UI does not imply full major-card support.

title: Route keyboard shortcut side effects through re-frame boundary
description:
Fresh-eye re-frame layer review: app.cljs owns a mutable keyboard-shortcut-listener atom at lines 1179-1180 and install-keyboard-shortcuts! directly adds/removes a window keydown listener at lines 1197-1226. The listener decides shortcut semantics and dispatches app events, while mount! installs it outside the re-frame event/fx layer at lines 1228-1230. This works, but it leaves browser side-effect lifecycle and the visible hotkey command model split across view code instead of an explicit effect boundary.

Acceptance criteria:
- Move global shortcut registration/cleanup into a focused browser effect/controller namespace or re-frame fx-style boundary.
- Keep shortcut definitions data-driven so the command dialog, listener, and smoke expectations share one source.
- Ensure reload/mount does not leak listeners.
- Preserve ?, G, I, Esc, and board-local keyboard behavior.
close_reason: Implemented shared shortcut catalog and re-frame effect listener boundary; tests, release, and smoke pass.

title: Make re-frame event handlers deterministic with injected randomness
description:
Fresh-eye re-frame layer review: reg-event-db handlers should be pure app-db transitions, but initialization and draw confirmation can reach default shuffle. src/main/gnostica/app.cljs registers ::initialize with reg-event-db at lines 14-17, app_state/initialize calls game-state/create-game at lines 130-138, and game_state/ordered-deck defaults shuffle-fn to shuffle at lines 164-169. Draw confirmation goes through app_state/confirmed-move-result at lines 1232-1235, while game_state/apply-draw-move defaults shuffle-fn to shuffle at lines 1742-1745. That puts nondeterminism inside db event handling instead of a coeffect/effect boundary.

Acceptance criteria:
- No reg-event-db path invokes default randomness implicitly.
- Introduce an explicit cofx/effects boundary, seed, or precomputed deck/shuffle-fn for initialization and discard reshuffles.
- Keep pure game-state helpers usable from tests with explicit shuffle inputs.
- Add tests that prove app event handling is deterministic when given the same injected inputs.
close_reason: Implemented explicit seeded shuffle cofx boundary for initialize and draw confirmation

title: Sun Cup-then-Disc transition is unreachable from browser move selection
description: The docs describe gnostica.game-state/apply-sun-move as the pure Sun facade for Cup followed by optional Disc, including same-created-target shortcuts. The move panel only chooses among :cup, :rod, and :disc and confirmed-move-result dispatches those to apply-cup-move, apply-rod-move, or apply-disc-move. A Sun source can be played as a separate Cup or Disc, but cannot execute the actual ordered Sun rule or its shortcuts from the browser UI. Evidence: README.md line 51, AGENTS.md line 31, MIND_MAP.md node [46], src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc move-power-order and confirmed-move-result, and game_state_test Sun coverage around apply-sun-move. Add Sun-specific staging/confirmation or explicitly mark Sun as pure-only/unavailable in the move panel and docs.
close_reason: Exposed Sun as explicit unavailable browser move power and documented current pure-only status.

title: Represent Cup major-arcana variants separately from plain Cup
description: src/main/gnostica/cards.cljc treats :cup-unbounded, :wheel-cup, and :wild-suits as plain Cup sources. The icon guide describes Empress ignoring the three-piece limit and Wheel optionally creating territory from the top draw-pile card, but app-state/apply-cup-move only expose normal Cup placement and hand one-point-card territory creation. Acceptance: carry the selected icon/variant through move commands, support or reject variant-specific options explicitly, and update docs/tests.
close_reason: done

title: Move Three.js runtime detection out of view render path
description:
Fresh-eye re-frame layer review: board-stage reads browser globals during view rendering by calling three-board/runtime-status, three-board/three-revision, and three-board/available? at app.cljs lines 464-467. Those helpers read js/THREE and OrbitControls directly in three_board.cljs lines 41-78. Re-frame views should mostly render subscription data; runtime capability probing is an environment/coeffect concern and should not be recomputed inside normal render.

Acceptance criteria:
- Capture Three.js runtime status through an explicit startup/lifecycle event, cofx, or effect boundary.
- Store renderer capability/status in app-db and expose it through subscriptions.
- Keep fallback rendering driven by subscription data rather than direct js global reads in views.
- Preserve current missing-global and revision-mismatch smoke behavior.
close_reason: Moved Three.js runtime detection into app-db board view data

title: Add hotkey to hide card icons with hover descriptions
description:
Goal: add a keyboard-accessible option to hide the always-visible Gnostica/special-move card icon overlays and instead reveal them on card hover/focus with brief descriptions.

Requirements:
- Add a hotkey or keyboard-accessible toggle that switches card icons between always-visible overlays and hidden-by-default popup mode.
- In popup mode, hovering or focusing a card should show that card special-move icons with concise text descriptions from the shared icon metadata.
- Preserve board, hand, discard, Three.js, and CSS fallback usability; hidden icons should not obscure card art until requested.
- Ensure keyboard users can discover or trigger the same descriptions without relying only on mouse hover.
- Keep icon labels sourced from gnostica.icons rather than duplicating text in renderer-specific code.
- Update tests or smoke coverage for the new mode where practical.

Acceptance criteria:
- A user can toggle card icon overlays off via hotkey or equivalent keyboard-accessible control.
- Hovering or focusing a card in the new mode shows the icons and brief descriptions for one-, two-, and three-icon major cards.
- Normal always-visible icon rendering remains available.
- Three.js and CSS fallback paths remain usable, with no incoherent overlap or layout shift.
close_reason: done

title: Implement standalone draw/discard turn transition
description: Add a browser-free gameplay transition for the standalone draw/discard turn option mentioned in README, AGENTS, and MIND_MAP. Acceptance: transition returns structured success/error maps, draws from draw pile into current player's hand or discard depending on rule interpretation from docs/rules.txt, preserves deck accounting and schema invariants, app-state confirmation uses the pure transition instead of an unavailable result, tests cover success and rejection, and docs/mind map are updated.
close_reason: Implemented pure draw/discard transition, wired draw confirmations through app-state, added tests, and updated README/AGENTS/MIND_MAP.

title: Render and expose pieces that occupy wasteland spaces
description: Gameplay can place and move pieces into wasteland spaces, for example initial placement can create {:space {:kind :wasteland ...}}. The board renderers only group and render pieces by :space-index: gnostica.pieces/pieces-by-space groups wasteland pieces under nil, the Three.js scene only adds piece meshes when a cell exists for (:space-index piece), and the CSS fallback only draws markers on board-card elements. Result: a legal wasteland piece is present in board-pieces but invisible and hard to inspect. Acceptance: represent board and wasteland piece occupancy by stable space coordinates, render wasteland piece markers/meshes in both 3D and CSS fallback paths, expose them in relevant panel data, and add tests/smoke coverage for initial placement or Rod movement into a wasteland.
close_reason: Implemented stable territory/wasteland piece space keys, rendered wasteland pieces in Three.js and CSS fallback, and added unit plus smoke coverage.

title: Draw/discard move panel cannot select discards at a full hand
description: README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md describe the standalone draw/discard turn as calling apply-draw-move, including optional discard-card-ids and drawing after selected discards. The browser move-selection state only requires draw-count, has no event/control path for selecting discard-card-ids, and max-draw-count ignores cards the player could discard. Result: at the normal six-card hand, the Draw cards source is disabled, so the UI cannot perform the basic rules action to discard any or none and then draw. Evidence: README.md lines 43, 45, and 61. AGENTS.md lines 35 and 47. src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc move-source-definitions, max-draw-count, draw-count-options, and move-command. Add discard-card selection state, controls, command building, and tests, or narrow the docs if this remains intentionally pure-only.
close_reason: Implemented draw discard-card selection in the move panel

title: Separate smoke runner CDP harness from Gnostica assertions
description:
Fresh-eye cohesion review: test/gnostica/smoke_runner.clj is 1125 lines and combines Chrome process management, DevTools protocol transport, JS snippets, screenshot analysis, input dispatch, server startup, and Gnostica board assertions. The Gnostica-specific scenario in run-happy-viewport! around lines 929-1032 sits on top of a reusable browser harness, but both layers are in one namespace.

Acceptance criteria:
- Extract CDP/Chrome lifecycle, command/evaluate/wait/input helpers into a test support namespace.
- Move page-stat JavaScript snippets and readiness predicates into focused Gnostica smoke modules or data structures.
- Leave smoke_runner.clj as high-level scenario orchestration.
- Preserve current clojure -M:smoke behavior and diagnostics.
close_reason: done

title: Harden pinned CDN script loading for Three.js
description:
Problem: `src/main/resources/index.html` loads Three.js and OrbitControls from pinned jsDelivr URLs at runtime, but there is no subresource integrity, crossorigin policy, local fallback, or automated check that the fetched bytes still match the expected r128 global build. The app has a CSS fallback when globals are absent, but release reproducibility and offline/corporate-network behavior still depend on a third-party CDN.

Evidence:
- `index.html` references `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/three@0.128.0/...` directly before `/js/main.js`.
- `src/main/externs/three.ext.js` is tied to the r128 global API, so accidental CDN or add-on drift can compile successfully but fail at runtime.

Acceptance criteria:
- Choose a hardened loading strategy that preserves the npm-free workflow: SRI plus crossorigin, vendored pinned scripts, or a documented local fallback path.
- Verify Three.js and OrbitControls are on the same r128 release line and fail visibly if a mismatched global is loaded.
- Add or update an automated/smoke check that detects missing or mismatched CDN globals before user interaction.
- Document the chosen strategy in README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md.
- `clojure -M:release` succeeds after the change with advanced-compilation externs still aligned.
close_reason: Added SRI/crossorigin to pinned Three.js CDN scripts, gated the runtime on r128 plus OrbitControls, added missing and mismatched global smoke coverage, and documented the strategy.

title: Shuffle the deck before dealing table cards
description:
Board setup currently deals the first nine cards from the ordered deck into the 3x3 territory grid. Add a shuffle step before dealing cards onto the table so each new table uses a randomized deck order.

Acceptance criteria:
- Shuffle the tarot deck before selecting the nine table/territory cards.
- Preserve existing board invariants: nine face-up cards, 3x3 placement, and alternating portrait/landscape orientations.
- Keep the shuffle path testable, preferably by injecting or seeding randomness instead of hard-coding global random state.
- Update or add tests that prove the deal no longer depends on the unshuffled deck order while the existing layout rules still hold.
close_reason: done

title: Fade board velvet into black beyond wastelands
description:
Adjust the board background so the velvet colour under and around territory cards fades to black with distance from the playable area. The velvet should remain visible under the territory cards and wasteland outlines, but by the time the surface extends past the wasteland squares and roughly halfway into the void it should be black.

Acceptance criteria:
- Three.js board rendering uses a distance-based fade or equivalent material/texture so the central territory area remains velvet and the outer void reaches black at the specified distance.
- CSS fallback board background matches the same visual intent closely enough that wasteland squares sit on velvet and farther void space reads as black.
- The fade respects current board-space/wasteland layout helpers instead of hard-coding only the initial 3x3 board dimensions where avoidable.
- Territory cards, wasteland outlines, pieces, card selection highlights, and icon popovers remain readable against the darker outer surface.
- Desktop and mobile smoke checks continue to see nonblank board content and dark table/void pixels; update smoke expectations if existing pixel thresholds assume uniform velvet.
- Update MIND_MAP.md or README.md if renderer behavior or smoke assertions change materially.
close_reason: Implemented the Three.js and CSS board fade, updated smoke checks and docs.

title: Tighten game schema invariants for impossible gameplay states
description: The Malli GameState schema currently accepts several states that the pure gameplay contract says should be impossible. Confirmed examples: a piece map with both :space-index and :space validates because Piece is an open :or map; a piece in {:space {:kind :wasteland :row 99 :col 99}} validates even when that coordinate is not a current wasteland; and [:setup :target-score] accepts any positive int such as 99 despite create-game allowing only 8, 9, or 10. Acceptance: require exactly one piece location shape, validate wasteland piece coordinates against board-layout/wasteland-spaces, restrict target score to game-state/allowed-target-scores, and surface specific invariant errors for these cases.
close_reason: Implemented schema invariants for piece locations, wasteland coordinates, and official target scores.

title: Clarify AGENTS test command comment includes feature scenarios
description: AGENTS.md:15 says clojure -M:test runs Clojure tests for shared code, while AGENTS.md:45 and README.md:91 say the same command also runs custom Gherkin-style gameplay feature scenarios. The command summary should not make feature failures look outside the normal test path. Acceptance: update the command comment to match README.md and test/gnostica/test_runner.clj.
close_reason: Updated AGENTS.md test command comment to include gameplay feature scenarios.

title: Implement Cup enemy-piece creation targets
description: docs/rules.txt Cups section allows a Cup to create an enemy small piece when targeting an enemy piece, preserving the enemy piece orientation. apply-cup-move only supports creating the current player's small piece on a target territory and its command target has no piece-target variant. README.md and AGENTS.md document the narrower behavior, so the current Cup slice omits a legal Cup branch. Acceptance: add explicit command/UI support and tests, or document the branch as unsupported in README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md.
close_reason: Implemented Cup enemy-piece creation targets

title: Render Gnostica icons on major arcana cards
description: Add Gnostica icon triplets to major arcana card rendering, using the Gnostica Stickers reference at https://www.wunderland.com/icehouse/GnosticaStickas.pdf. The PDF describes 22 icon triplets for the major arcana, placed on the upper-left corner of the correct major card. Acceptance: every major arcana card rendered by the app shows the correct icon triplet consistently across board, hand, and card-zone surfaces; minor arcana rendering is unchanged; and the implementation uses maintainable source data and assets instead of hard-coded view-only exceptions.
close_reason: Implemented major arcana icon triplets across shared card metadata, DOM card views, Three.js textures, and verification.

title: Render major arcana icons on Three.js card textures
description: Add the same major arcana icon triplets to the primary Three.js board cards. Acceptance: generated card textures or overlay meshes include the upper-left triplet on major cards, texture loading and the r128 runtime gate keep working, icons are visible on desktop and mobile board views, selection and raycasting behavior is unchanged, and missing icon assets produce an explicit renderer error instead of a silent blank overlay.
close_reason: Implemented major arcana icon metadata, scalable renderers, Three.js canvas textures, DOM overlays, and verification.

title: Render major arcana icons in DOM card views
description: Render the major arcana icon triplets on 2D card surfaces such as hand cards, card-zone previews, and the CSS fallback board. Acceptance: major cards show the ordered triplet in the upper-left overlay, the overlay scales with card size without covering titles or important imagery, minor cards remain visually unchanged, and DOM rendering uses the shared card metadata and icon assets rather than duplicating per-card conditionals.
close_reason: Implemented major arcana icon metadata, scalable renderers, Three.js canvas textures, DOM overlays, and verification.

title: Create scalable Gnostica icon assets
description: Add the visual assets needed to render the Stickas major arcana icon triplets. Acceptance: icons are repository-native scalable assets or components suitable for both DOM and canvas/texture rendering, the asset strategy avoids depending on cropped PDF raster images unless rights and quality are explicitly accepted, each icon has a stable id matching the metadata contract, and the icons remain legible at small card-overlay sizes.
close_reason: Implemented major arcana icon metadata, scalable renderers, Three.js canvas textures, DOM overlays, and verification.

title: Extract major arcana icon triplet metadata
description: Create the browser-free data contract for Gnostica major arcana icon triplets from the Stickas reference. Acceptance: all 22 major arcana card ids have exactly three ordered icon ids, card metadata exposes the triplet without coupling to Reagent or Three.js, tests fail if a major arcana card is missing icons or a minor arcana card gets major-only icons, and the source reference is noted in code or docs where maintainers will find it.
close_reason: Implemented major arcana icon metadata, scalable renderers, Three.js canvas textures, DOM overlays, and verification.

title: Verify major arcana icon rendering
description: Add focused verification for the major arcana icon overlay work. Acceptance: Clojure tests cover the metadata completeness and card lookup invariants, browser or smoke checks confirm at least one major card renders its icon triplet in both Three.js and CSS fallback paths, release compilation still succeeds with any new externs or assets, and the verification notes mention the Stickas PDF as the visual source.
close_reason: Implemented major arcana icon metadata, scalable renderers, Three.js canvas textures, DOM overlays, and verification.

title: Track browser move-selection gaps for implemented major powers
description: Pure browser-free facades now exist for Fool, High Priestess, Judgement, World, and the ordered multi-action/composite major flows, but the browser move-selection power list only exposes :cup, :rod, :disc, :sun, :sword, :hierophant, :hermit, and :devil. Some major cards are usable only as single suit subsets, and World/draw-major powers are unavailable from the browser despite implemented pure transitions. Acceptance: either add a clear browser-support epic with child tickets for these remaining staged controls or wire the missing flows directly; keep explicit unavailable results for powers still intentionally unwired so implemented pure rules do not disappear from product planning.
close_reason: Tracked remaining browser support under bd-ydt with child tickets for draw majors, ordered composite suit majors, Sword-major composites, World delegation, and power-choice/unavailable-state audit.

title: Split app.cljs browser shell into focused view modules
description:
Fresh-eye cohesion review: src/main/gnostica/app.cljs is 1263 lines and acts as re-frame registration, CSS fallback board renderer, shared card renderer, card zones, territory panel, move panel, help dialogs, keyboard shortcuts, startup, and smoke init hook. The first 300 lines are event/sub registration, while later sections contain board rendering, move controls, dialogs, global key handling, and init logic. Since domain state is already delegated to app_state and 3D rendering to three_board, this namespace is carrying too many browser responsibilities.

Acceptance criteria:
- Move board fallback/card-zone/territory-panel/move-panel/help-dialog views into focused UI namespaces.
- Keep re-frame event/sub registration either in a thin wiring namespace or colocated with clear feature boundaries.
- Keep gnostica.app as the browser shell/startup entry point.
- Preserve existing subscriptions/events and smoke-visible DOM attributes.
close_reason: done

title: Add gameplay move selection controls
description:
Goal: build the user interface flow that lets a player choose and confirm legal gameplay moves once the pure gameplay state and rule transitions are available.

Requirements:
- Add a move-selection mode to the app UI that is driven by shared gameplay state rather than renderer-only board state.
- Let the current player choose the move source, such as activating a board territory, playing a hand card, drawing cards, orienting a piece, or placing an initial small piece when they have no pieces on the board.
- Guide the player through required targets and parameters for the selected move: card, piece/minion, target space or piece, orientation, hand-card choice, draw count, and confirmation/cancel states as applicable.
- Surface only currently legal choices when the rule engine can provide them, and show clear disabled/error states for rejected transitions.
- Keep Three.js board selection, CSS fallback selection, territory panel, and future hand/player panels synchronized through re-frame events/subscriptions.
- Make the flow keyboard and pointer accessible enough for repeated gameplay, with predictable focus and no reliance on hover-only controls.
- Preserve deterministic, testable boundaries: UI events should call pure transition helpers and render their explicit success/error results.

Acceptance criteria:
- The UI has a clear current-player move picker and staged selection controls for source, target, options, confirm, and cancel.
- Selecting board cards and pieces feeds the active move flow instead of only updating the inspection panel.
- Invalid or incomplete selections cannot silently mutate state; errors are visible and recoverable.
- Tests cover the re-frame event/subscription state machine where practical, and manual smoke notes cover board/card/piece selection integration.
- README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md are updated if new UI state namespaces, commands, or gameplay verification steps are added.

Implementation notes:
- This should follow the shared gameplay state/player setup/rule-transition work closely enough that the UI can consume authoritative move legality instead of duplicating rules.
- Prefer small reusable controls for move source selection, target prompts, orientation selection, and confirmation actions.
close_reason: Implemented move-selection UI flow with app-state tests, docs, release build, and smoke verification.

title: Recompress MIND_MAP after recent expansions
description:
MIND_MAP.md line 3 asks agents to keep the map compact (20-50 nodes typical) and line-oriented, but the file now has 53 nodes and several very large single-line nodes, especially [24], [29], [37], [38], [46], and [47]. This makes grep output and review diffs hard to use. A previous compaction ticket was closed before the latest expansions, so this is current drift rather than an open duplicate.

Acceptance: consolidate, split, or rebalance nodes so the map stays within the stated compact target or deliberately revise the target; keep [6-10] as practical anchors, preserve [1-5], and reduce extreme node lengths while retaining useful cross-links.

title: Add local lobby for player setup before game start
description:
Add a first-screen local lobby flow before creating the game. This is scoped to the current single-browser app: players join locally, enter a display name, choose an available player colour, and then start the game. Networked multiplayer sync remains out of scope unless a separate ticket adds it.

Acceptance criteria:
- App initialization can start in a lobby state without immediately creating :game.
- Lobby UI lets two to six players join, edit their display names, choose unique colours from gnostica.pieces/players metadata, remove joined players before start, and start only when setup is valid.
- Starting the game calls the existing gnostica.game-state/create-game path with the selected player ids/names while preserving deterministic shuffle injection through the app event/cofx boundary.
- Existing demo/smoke init options can still bypass or prefill the lobby so current verification flows remain deterministic.
- Header, board, card-zone, territory, and move-panel views handle the pre-game lobby state without nil-game errors.
- Add app-state and UI-facing tests for lobby validation, duplicate colour rejection, player count limits, name persistence, start-game initialization, and cancellation/removal behavior.
- Update README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md if the startup flow, app-db shape, or smoke workflow changes.
close_reason: Implemented in the current codebase and verified with clojure -M:test.

title: Harden create-game validation for player and deck inputs
description:
Problem: `gnostica.game-state/create-game` validates player count, missing ids, duplicate ids, and minimum deck size, but it accepts player ids with no known piece/player metadata and accepts any deck-like collection with at least nine entries. That leaves the authoritative state boundary open to malformed states before Malli/schema work exists. It also exposes a rule mismatch: setup claims two to six players, while `gnostica.pieces/players` currently defines only four color/player records.

Evidence:
- `normalize-player` merges optional `pieces/players-by-id` data and then the caller supplied player spec, so unknown ids can enter state with incomplete color metadata.
- `validate-deck` checks only `(count deck) >= board/board-card-count`, not uniqueness or card shape.
- Existing tests reject too few or too many players but do not cover unknown ids, five/six-player metadata, duplicate cards, or malformed deck entries.

Acceptance criteria:
- Decide and enforce whether player ids must be from known player metadata or whether custom player metadata is required for unknown ids.
- Two-through-six-player setup either has complete player/color metadata for every legal player count or returns structured validation errors explaining the unsupported players.
- Deck validation catches duplicate card ids and malformed/missing card data needed by setup and rendering.
- Validation failures use the existing explicit result map shape with stable error codes and useful `:data`.
- Tests cover unknown player ids, five/six-player setup behavior, duplicate deck entries, malformed cards, and the existing happy path.
close_reason: Hardened create-game player/deck validation, added six player metadata, expanded tests, and documented the stricter boundary.

title: Drive browser state from the shared game-state contract
description:
Problem: README.md and AGENTS.md identify `gnostica.game-state/create-game` as the authoritative browser-free gameplay state boundary, but `gnostica.app/::initialize` still builds a separate renderer-oriented state with `board/initial-board`, `pieces/initial-pieces`, and `:selected-board-index`. That creates a second setup path that bypasses explicit success/error results, player metadata, turn order, draw/discard state, setup/bid metadata, and future rule transitions.

Evidence:
- `src/main/gnostica/app.cljs` initializes top-level `:board` and `:pieces` directly.
- `src/main/gnostica/game_state.cljc` already provides deterministic `create-game` results intended for re-frame and tests.

Acceptance criteria:
- Browser initialization creates and stores an authoritative game state through `gnostica.game-state/create-game` for the default/demo players, or renders an explicit setup error if creation fails.
- Board, piece, selected territory, and current-player subscriptions derive from the shared game state rather than parallel renderer-only top-level state.
- Any demo-only visible piece fixtures are either represented through the gameplay state contract or isolated and named as non-game fixtures.
- Focused tests cover the initialization path and at least one selection/update path against the new state shape.
- README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md are updated if the app-db shape or verification workflow changes.
close_reason: Browser app initialization now stores the shared create-game state under :game, derives board/piece/current-player subscriptions from it, covers the app-db path with focused tests, and updates README/AGENTS/MIND_MAP.

title: Return pieces to stash when territory relocation leaves them in the void
description: Rod territory pushes and Hermit territory moves leave source-coordinate pieces behind by converting pieces on the moved territory to {:space {:kind :wasteland :row ... :col ...}} before moving the card. Unlike Sword territory destruction, these paths do not re-check whether that old coordinate is still a wasteland after the card moves. In a disconnected or gapped board, moving a territory from [0,0] to a wasteland at [0,2] adjacent to another territory can leave the old [0,0] coordinate in the void while the piece remains active. Acceptance: after any territory relocation, return pieces at coordinates that are no longer a territory or current wasteland to their owner stashes, keep stash mirrors reconciled, and cover both Rod territory push and Hermit territory move.
close_reason: done

title: Change board ground to a velvet color
description:
Goal: make the surface beneath the territory cards feel more like a velvet play mat instead of a neutral ground.

Requirements:
- Change the 3D board ground/table surface under the cards to a velvet-toned color.
- Preserve card readability, piece contrast, lighting balance, and existing board spacing.
- Keep the change visual-only: do not alter board layout, card picking, camera controls, or gameplay state.
- Consider whether the CSS fallback board background should use a matching velvet color for consistency.
- Avoid adding image or texture dependencies unless a flat material cannot produce the desired result.

Acceptance criteria:
- The 3D board renders with a clearly velvet-colored ground beneath the cards.
- Cards and Icehouse pieces remain legible against the new surface.
- Existing tests and release compilation still pass.
- A smoke or screenshot-based check covers the changed board background if practical.
close_reason: Implemented burgundy velvet 3D and fallback board ground with smoke coverage.

title: Use board cell indexes instead of vector positions in app-state and move-selection
description: Fresh-eye review found that app-state and move-selection often treat :board as a positional vector with get/contains? even though gameplay cells carry stable :index values. Sword territory destruction removes cells and leaves index gaps, so selecting board index 5 after index 4 is destroyed returns the cell with :index 6; higher valid indexes can be rejected entirely. Affects selected-board-cell/select-board-card, move-selection target/source lookup, piece coordinate labels, and option derivation. Acceptance: centralize board-index lookup by :index for app-state subscriptions, move-selection, and UI labels; add coverage for selecting/targeting a surviving territory after a territory is destroyed and after creating a new territory in a gapped board.
close_reason: Fixed app-state and move-selection board lookups to use stable cell indexes with gapped-board coverage

title: Render pyramid piece edges with black outlines
description:
Goal: make Icehouse pyramid pieces easier to read on tarot cards by rendering visible black edge lines around each pyramid.

Requirements:
- Add a visual edge treatment to 3D pyramid meshes so their silhouettes and side-face boundaries remain visible against card art.
- Cover upright pieces and cardinal/lying pieces without changing existing piece position, orientation, or selection behavior.
- Prefer a Three.js-native approach such as edge geometry/line segments or an equivalent overlay that works with the current CDN r128 global build.
- Keep the CSS fallback behavior intact; add a matching fallback outline only if needed for visual consistency.
- Update externs if new Three.js APIs are used by advanced compilation.

Acceptance criteria:
- Pyramids render with visible black edge lines in the 3D board view.
- Existing piece orientation and height tests still pass, and release compilation succeeds.
- A smoke or focused rendering check covers at least one selected viewport where edge outlines are expected to be present.
close_reason: done

title: Mind map node [5] references a missing linked gist
description: MIND_MAP.md node [5] says the methodology includes a bootstrap prompt as a linked gist, but the node and surrounding file do not contain a gist URL or any actual link. This is small, but it weakens the self-documentation instructions because agents are told the reference exists when it does not. Evidence: MIND_MAP.md line 15. Add the link if it is still useful, or remove the phrase linked gist from the preserved generic node if that reference should not be part of this repository copy.
close_reason: Removed unsupported linked gist reference from MIND_MAP.md node [5].

title: Clarify card-zone docs now that Cup hand cards discard
description: README.md:41 and AGENTS.md:27 still imply drawing and discarding are entirely future pure gameplay transitions. But gnostica.game-state/apply-cup-move already removes a hand-source Cup card and app-state card-zone tests assert the discard pile updates. Clarify that the missing transition is the standalone draw/discard turn option, not every discard mutation, and keep README, AGENTS, and MIND_MAP wording aligned.
close_reason: Clarified card-zone docs for Cup hand-card discard behavior

title: pressing the I hotkey should not reset the view
close_reason: done

title: allow wasd style hotkey and arrow key movement of the camera/world
close_reason: done

title: Replace raw app-db subscriptions with composed view-model subscriptions
description:
Fresh-eye re-frame layer review: app.cljs registers most subscriptions as direct app-db query functions at lines 139-302, then views compose many raw subscriptions themselves. board-stage derives pieces-by-space, wastelands, and bounds in the view at lines 455-459. move-active-controls dereferences roughly sixteen subscriptions at lines 907-924 before deciding which controls to render. This blurs the subscription layer: derived data and view models are being assembled in Reagent render functions instead of a composed signal graph.

Acceptance criteria:
- Split subscriptions into extractor subscriptions and composed view-model subscriptions for board, card zones, move panel, and dialogs.
- Move board layout derivations and move-control option aggregation out of views and behind subscriptions where they are cached/reactive.
- Reduce high-level views to one or two feature-level subscriptions plus event dispatch callbacks.
- Preserve existing DOM output and app-state behavior.
close_reason: Implemented composed view-model subscriptions for board, card zones, move panel, dialogs, header, and app shell.

title: Represent Rod unbounded variants separately from plain Rod
description: src/main/gnostica/cards.cljc treats :rod-unbounded and :wild-suits as plain Rod sources. The icon guide documents unbounded Rod behavior as ignoring normal space-limit blockers, while resolve/apply-rod-command always enforce the ordinary full-destination check and has no variant field. Acceptance: carry the Rod variant into commands and implement the unbounded behavior or explicitly reject/document it until supported; add tests for full-space behavior.
close_reason: done

title: Make main board view fill the browser window
description: The primary game and board view should take the full available browser window in normal play. Secondary panels such as move controls, territory details, card zones, and help should open as overlays that hover above the main view and dock to the sides as needed instead of permanently reducing the main view's usable space. Acceptance criteria: the main board/game view fills the viewport; side panels can be opened and closed and render above the main view; panels avoid blocking core board interactions where practical and remain usable on desktop and mobile; existing Three.js and CSS fallback layouts continue to work.
close_reason: done

title: Document br issue tracking outside AGENTS
description:
AGENTS.md documents the br/beads_rust issue tracker, including br sync --flush-only and committing .beads changes with Jujutsu. README.md and MIND_MAP.md contain no issue-tracking guidance, even though tickets are repo artifacts under .beads and the mind map is the primary knowledge index for agents. Contributors or agents starting from README/MIND_MAP can miss the expected tracker and label workflow.

Acceptance: add concise issue-tracking guidance to README.md and a MIND_MAP node or link, keep AGENTS.md as the detailed source, mention br sync --flush-only plus .beads/Jujutsu handling, and avoid duplicating long CLI help.
close_reason: done

title: Implement or hide unavailable orient and initial-placement flows
description: README.md:55 and AGENTS.md:37 describe the move-selection flow, but src/main/gnostica/app_state.cljc exposes :orient-piece and :place-initial-small as selectable sources that can reach confirm and then fall through to :move-transition-unavailable. Initial small placement also stages only existing territories, while docs/rules.txt allows an empty territory or wasteland when a player has no pieces. Acceptance: implement pure transitions and UI targets, or keep these sources disabled/clearly unavailable until implemented; cover successful and rejected confirmations.
close_reason: Implemented orient and initial placement transitions

title: Define Swords command contract and card helpers
description: Introduce the browser-free command/action shape and reusable legality helpers for Swords. Acceptance: commands explicitly identify player id, source as territory or hand card, acting minion piece id, target as piece or territory, selected sword variant, damage amount, optional replacement-card source/id for territory attacks, and optional orientation when shrinking one of the current player's pieces. Card helpers identify Swords minor cards plus Sword-bearing majors such as Magician, Justice, Death, Tower, and Moon; target resolution follows docs/rules.txt targeting rules for upright/current-space, cardinal adjacent-space, and minion self-targeting; invalid commands return structured errors without mutating game state.
close_reason: Implemented Sword card helpers and browser-free resolve-sword-command contract with focused tests and docs

title: Unimplemented Sword-only sources are staged as Cup instead of unavailable
description: README.md says move sources without a pure transition return an explicit move-transition-unavailable result, and AGENTS.md asks for explicit unavailable results rather than silent mutation. Current move selection does not model Sword as an implemented power, but selected-power returns :cup when the selected source card has no Cup/Rod/Disc options. A Swords-only source therefore walks the user through Cup target controls and fails only after confirmation with source-card-not-cup. app_state_test currently codifies that behavior in rejected-cup-confirmation-keeps-staged-selection. Evidence: README.md line 61, AGENTS.md lines 33 and 47, src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc selected-power and move-power-order, test/gnostica/app_state_test.clj around the rejected Cup confirmation. Decide whether to expose Sword as unavailable, return move-transition-unavailable immediately, or implement Sword confirmation wiring, then update the tests and docs.
close_reason: Fixed Sword-only move sources to stage as Sword and confirm unavailable

title: Move pyramid pips to side faces near the base edge
description:
Goal: adjust Icehouse pyramid pip marker placement so pips sit on the side faces, next to the edge by the base of each pyramid.

Requirements:
- Move 3D pip markers from the current base-facing placement onto pyramid side faces near the base edge.
- Keep one, two, and three pip counts visually clear for small, medium, and large pieces.
- Preserve black piece edge outlines, piece color contrast, board/card readability, and existing piece orientation behavior.
- Update browser-free layout helpers and tests if the placement math remains pure.
- Keep the change visual-only: do not alter piece ownership, board layout, card picking, camera controls, or gameplay state.

Acceptance criteria:
- Upright and lying/cardinal pyramids show their pips along side faces next to the base edge.
- Pip counts remain legible at the default desktop and mobile board views.
- Existing tests, release compilation, and the 3D smoke check pass.
- A focused layout or smoke assertion covers the corrected pip placement if practical.
close_reason: done

title: Move demo and smoke fixtures out of production app flow
description:
Fresh-eye cohesion review: demo and verification fixtures are part of production namespaces. src/main/gnostica/pieces.cljc defines initial-pieces, app_state.cljc injects default demo board pieces during normal initialization, and app.cljs has the smoke-only URL/deck-order hook around lines 1235-1259. That couples domain models and browser startup to demo/smoke setup concerns.

Acceptance criteria:
- Introduce a fixture/demo namespace for seeded visible pieces and smoke deck orders.
- Make production initialization opt into fixtures through explicit init options instead of hard-coded defaults in core model/app namespaces.
- Keep default local demo behavior if desired, but make the production model and browser startup boundaries free of smoke-specific logic.
- Update smoke/app-state tests to use the fixture namespace directly.
close_reason: Implemented demo/smoke fixture extraction

title: Reconcile demo board pieces with stash accounting
description: README.md:41 and MIND_MAP.md:35 say demo pieces are kept in the authoritative :game [:pieces :on-board]. src/main/gnostica/app_state.cljc injects pieces/initial-pieces by associng :pieces :on-board, but player :stash values and [:pieces :stashes] remain full. Cup creation reads those stash counts, so the default app state can create extra small pieces and current schema/tests do not catch the mismatch. Acceptance: either remove demo pieces from authoritative gameplay setup, decrement both stash mirrors when seeding them, or make them renderer-only; add coverage for stash/piece count consistency.
close_reason: done

title: Clean up or configure clojure-lsp diagnostics warnings
description: Fresh diagnostics run completed tests/release successfully but clojure-lsp diagnostics exits nonzero because of warnings. Actionable examples include unused bindings in src/main/gnostica/game_state/disc.cljc:978 and src/main/gnostica/move_selection.cljc:230, unused private vars such as move_selection/discard-card-by-id and manipulation/source-card-id, an unused clojure.string require in fixtures.cljc, plus a redundant let in test/gnostica/smoke/browser.clj. Many unused-public-var infos are intentional facade/export shims and may need configuration rather than deletion. Acceptance: remove genuine dead bindings/vars/requires, configure intentional public facade exports, and make clojure-lsp diagnostics useful as a clean verification signal.
close_reason: Removed real clojure-lsp warnings and configured intentional unused-public-var infos off.

title: Display player hand, draw deck, and discard pile
description:
Goal: show the current player's hand of cards, the deck they draw from, and the discard pile in the browser UI.

Requirements:
- Derive hand, draw pile, and discard pile from the authoritative game state under app-db :game; do not duplicate card-zone state at the top level.
- Display the current player's six-card hand with card titles and images where practical, preserving responsive layout and readable card art.
- Display the draw deck as a distinct zone with at least remaining-card count and a clear facedown/deck affordance.
- Display the discard pile as a distinct zone with count and latest/top discarded card when present, plus an empty state when absent.
- Keep this UI-only unless pure gameplay transitions already exist; do not silently mutate :game for drawing or discarding.
- Ensure the card zones fit alongside the existing board, move panel, and territory panel on desktop and mobile without overlap.
- Update README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md if the app layout, subscriptions, or verification workflow changes.

Acceptance criteria:
- The current player's hand, draw deck, and discard pile are visible from the main game screen.
- Zone contents are derived from gnostica.game-state data and update when app-db :game changes.
- Empty and non-empty discard pile states render clearly.
- clojure -M:test passes, and release/smoke checks are updated or run if the visible layout changes.
close_reason: Implemented current-player hand, draw deck, and discard pile UI

title: Extract Three.js texture and icon canvas rendering
description:
Fresh-eye cohesion review: src/main/gnostica/three_board.cljs mixes scene lifecycle with a local Canvas icon renderer. It has icon drawing primitives and a 130-line draw-icon-mark! around lines 238-367 that parallels gnostica.icon-view.cljs, plus card image cover and canvas texture composition around lines 399-475. The board namespace now owns both visual asset generation and Three.js orchestration.

Acceptance criteria:
- Move card texture composition and Canvas icon drawing into a dedicated namespace, for example gnostica.three-card-textures or gnostica.icon-texture.
- Keep the Three.js board responsible for mesh/material hookup, not symbol drawing details.
- Add focused tests or smoke-visible metadata to guard icon count/layout parity with icon_view where practical.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Make board base colour dark velvet
description:
Goal: adjust the board base/table colour so the play surface reads as dark velvet.

Requirements:
- Update the 3D board base/table material colour to a dark velvet tone.
- Keep tarot card art, piece colours, pip markers, black outlines, and wasteland outlines readable against the darker base.
- Keep the CSS fallback board surface visually consistent with the 3D base colour.
- Update smoke/layout assertions or documented expected colour metadata if they currently pin the old base colour.
- Keep this visual-only: do not alter board layout, card picking, camera controls, or gameplay state.

Acceptance criteria:
- Desktop and mobile 3D board views show a dark velvet base behind the cards and pieces.
- CSS fallback paths use a matching dark velvet base.
- Existing tests, release compilation, and 3D smoke checks pass.
close_reason: Implemented dark velvet 3D and fallback board base color with updated smoke expectations

title: Add pip/notch size markers to pyramid pieces
description:
Goal: add small visual size markers near the base of every rendered Icehouse/Looney pyramid so players can identify size at a glance without relying only on mesh height.

Background: Looney pyramid pieces use pips as their size convention. Looney Labs says each piece is marked with 1, 2, or 3 pips, and the Pyramid Arcade guide defines pips as teardrop-shaped marks: smalls have one pip, mediums two pips, larges three pips. Wired similarly notes small pips near the base in counts of 1, 2, and 3.

Requirements:
- Add visible notch/pip-style marks near the base of pyramid pieces: one for small, two for medium, three for large.
- Apply the markers to the primary Three.js pyramid meshes and keep them visible enough from the default board camera.
- Update the CSS fallback piece rendering with matching size markers.
- Keep markers aligned with piece color/lighting/orientation and avoid interfering with selection raycasts or board/card picking.
- Prefer a Looney-pyramid-inspired mark, but avoid copying exact proprietary piece geometry beyond the general one/two/three pip convention.

Acceptance criteria:
- Small, medium, and large pieces are visually distinguishable by marker count in both 3D and CSS fallback views.
- Upright and cardinal/lying pieces still render with correct orientation and board placement.
- Existing piece tests remain green, and new focused tests or smoke notes cover marker count mapping if practical.
- README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md are updated if renderer behavior or verification guidance changes.

Reference details:
- https://www.looneylabs.com/pyramids-equipment
- https://www.looneylabs.com/sites/default/files/literature/Pyramids_Instruction_Guide.pdf
- https://www.wired.com/2013/01/looney-pyramids/
close_reason: Implemented 3D and CSS pip markers with tests and docs

title: background velvet to be darker
close_reason: done

title: Refresh MIND_MAP rules-source anchor beyond board setup
description: MIND_MAP.md node [7] still frames board setup as the implemented rule slice, but the current code and later nodes also cover six-card hands, draw/discard zones, turn metadata, and Cup move transitions. As an anchor node, this can send agents into an outdated mental model. Update [7] to summarize the current implemented rule slices and link to the game-state/Cup nodes.
close_reason: Updated MIND_MAP rules-source anchor to cover current implemented setup, card-zone, turn, and Cup rule slices.

title: Make MIND_MAP opening guidance point agents at Gnostica nodes
description: MIND_MAP.md:3 tells agents to read overview nodes 1-5 first and treats them as the primary knowledge index, but nodes 1-5 are generic mind-map format documentation while the actual Gnostica overview starts at node 6. Adjust the guidance or add project overview anchors so agents do not spend the first pass on format mechanics instead of repo facts. Preserve any intentional format rules explicitly.
close_reason: done

title: Fix MIND_MAP navigation wording around format nodes and project anchors
description: MIND_MAP.md:3 tells agents to start at project anchors [6-10] and consult [1-5] only for format rules, but node [5] says overview nodes [1-5] serve as navigation hubs. That generic format text conflicts with this file's project navigation model and could send agents back into format nodes instead of project anchors. Acceptance: reconcile the wording without breaking the first-five-node convention, or add a project-specific clarification outside immutable format nodes.
close_reason: done

title: Increase special move icon size
description: Increase the rendered size of the special move/Gnostica card icons, using an initial target of 100% larger than the current size. Acceptance: DOM card overlays and Three.js generated card textures both use the larger icon scale; one-, two-, and three-icon cards remain legible without clipping or incoherent overlap; the size change is centralized where practical rather than duplicated per renderer; tests or smoke coverage are updated where existing icon rendering checks depend on sizing.
close_reason: done

title: Automate 3D board smoke verification
description:
Problem: README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md describe important browser smoke checks for the 3D board, but the repository currently has only shared Clojure tests. High-risk behavior such as CDN global loading, WebGL canvas nonblank rendering, texture load failures, orbit/reset controls, mobile sizing, CSS fallback behavior, and raycast selection is still documented as manual or optional automation.

Evidence:
- `clojure -M:test` covers cards, board, pieces, and game-state data only.
- The documented verification checklist includes desktop/mobile canvas and interaction checks that are not encoded in a repeatable command.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add a documented smoke command that can run against a released or dev-served app without making npm part of the default workflow.
- The smoke verifies desktop and mobile viewport widths, a nonblank Three.js canvas, absence of texture/fallback errors in the happy path, visible board content, reset control presence, and selection updating the territory panel.
- Include a fallback-path check for missing WebGL or missing Three globals if the chosen harness can simulate it reliably.
- The command reports actionable failures with enough context to diagnose CDN, asset, extern, or renderer lifecycle regressions.
- README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md name the command and clarify when it should be run.
close_reason: Added clojure -M:smoke browser verification for the 3D board and documented it.

title: Significantly increase board view brightness
description:
Goal: make the current board view substantially brighter and easier to read.

Requirements:
- Brighten the Three.js board scene so card textures are not dimmed by material colors.
- Increase surrounding board/UI contrast enough that the app no longer reads as very dark overall.
- Preserve the existing tarot image assets, 3D layout, fallback board behavior, and npm-free workflow.
- Keep text and controls readable after the brightness increase.

Acceptance criteria:
- The 3D card faces render at full or near-full texture brightness.
- The table/canvas and adjacent UI are visibly brighter than the previous dark treatment.
- clojure -M:test passes.
- clojure -M:release succeeds with 0 shadow-cljs compiler warnings.
close_reason: Brightened Three.js board rendering with white card materials, sRGB texture/output encoding, brighter table colors, and a lighter UI palette.

title: Render lying-down pyramids flat against card faces
description: Adjust the Three.js Icehouse pyramid orientation for pieces in a lying-down/cardinal position. A lying pyramid should first be rotated about its long axis by 45 degrees so one side, not an edge, is facing down. It should then receive the small corrective rotation needed for that side plane to be completely parallel to the card face, so the pyramid visually lies flat on the card instead of balancing or clipping awkwardly. Acceptance: lying/cardinal pyramid meshes rest flush on the textured card plane, keep their intended compass direction, do not visibly sink through the card, and still select the underlying territory normally.
close_reason: done

title: hotkey to display more detailed explanation of all special move icons
close_reason: Added G hotkey and special move icon guide

title: allow zooming in to go in closer than the current limit
close_reason: Lowered the 3D OrbitControls minimum zoom distance to 3.2 and added smoke coverage for the configured zoom bounds.

title: Disambiguate deck shuffling and deal order in docs
description: MIND_MAP.md node [8] says board/initial-board shuffles before taking nine table cards, while the authoritative create-game path shuffles or orders the full deck once, deals player hands first, then calls board/initial-board with identity on the remaining board deck. Clarify the direct helper behavior versus the create-game setup path so future changes do not double-shuffle or deal the board before hands.
close_reason: Clarified direct board helper shuffle versus create-game deal order in README, AGENTS, and mind map docs.

title: Epic: Complete remaining Gnostica gameplay rules
description:
Track the gameplay rules still missing or only partially wired after the implemented setup, draw/discard, Cup, Rod, Disc, single Sword, orient-piece, and initial-placement flows.

Scope comes from README.md, AGENTS.md, MIND_MAP.md, and docs/rules.txt. The biggest gaps are game lifecycle rules, official bidding, full major-arcana sequencing, browser Sun staging, deferred Sword-major composites, and remaining major arcana powers.
close_reason: All gameplay rules child tickets completed and documentation reconciled

title: Implement scoring, challenge, victory, and elimination
description:
Implement the winning rules from docs/rules.txt: scores are based on territory occupied only by one players pieces, target score defaults to 9 with optional 8 or 10 point games, players may announce a final-turn challenge, and that challenge resolves after their next turn as either a win or elimination.

Current state has score, challenge, and target-score fields, but no full rule transition or browser flow.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Add browser-free scoring helpers: spot territories count 1, royalty 2, major 3, wasteland 0.
- Score only territories occupied exclusively by the scoring players pieces; contested or enemy-only territories do not count.
- Add an end-turn challenge command or transition that records a pending final turn and rejects a second pending challenge.
- Resolve a pending challenge after that players next turn: reaching target score wins, missing it eliminates the player.
- Elimination removes all of that players pieces from board and wastelands and discards their hand while preserving card and stash accounting.
- Support target scores 8, 9, and 10 from setup options or lobby options.
- Cover pure state, schema invariants, app-state confirmation, and feature scenarios.
close_reason: done

title: Implement official bid-to-start setup flow
description:
Implement the official starting-player rule from docs/rules.txt: players secretly bid a card, bids are revealed, highest numbered major wins if any major is bid, otherwise highest minor wins by king through ace order, ties are set aside and rebid, bid cards are redrawn counterclockwise starting with the player to the right of the winner, and the winner goes first.

Current setup records bid metadata but game creation starts directly with deterministic turn order.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Add pure bidding state transitions for selecting bid cards, revealing bids, tie rebids, and finalizing the starting player.
- Major bid comparison follows tarot major numbering; minor comparison follows king, queen, knight, page, ten through ace.
- Tied winning bids are set aside and unavailable until the bid resolves.
- After a winner is selected, players redraw from bid cards in counterclockwise order starting right of the winner until hands return to six or bid cards are exhausted.
- Winner becomes current player and turn order proceeds clockwise from that player.
- Preserve unique card accounting across hands, bid set-aside cards, draw pile, discard pile, and board.
- Add feature scenarios for major win, minor win, tie and rebid, and redraw order.
close_reason: Implemented official browser-free starting bid flow

title: Add major-arcana sequencing engine
description:
Add shared browser-free support for ordered optional multi-power major arcana actions. Rules include hand-play sources making all owned pieces minions, territory activation using pieces touching that card, affected owned pieces becoming minions for later powers in that turn, source card cost charged once, optional powers, fixed power order, and same-target shortcut hooks.

This should provide the reusable foundation for Lovers, Chariot, Justice, Hanged Man, Death, Temperance, Moon, Sun browser staging, and similar major-card flows.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Define a stable command shape for ordered major-arcana action sequences with explicit source, selected actions, minions, targets, and optional shortcut markers.
- Validate that action order matches the card text and cannot be reversed.
- Allow players to omit later optional powers while preserving the rule that selected powers execute in order.
- Charge a hand-source major card exactly once even when multiple sub-actions run.
- For board activation, track eligible minions from pieces on the activated territory plus owned pieces directly affected by earlier sub-actions.
- Preserve existing single-action Cup, Rod, Disc, Sword, orient, and placement transition contracts.
- Add pure tests for order rejection, source-cost-once behavior, minion eligibility propagation, and no-mutation-on-failure.
close_reason: Added browser-free major arcana sequencing engine with source resolution, single source cost, ordered optional actions, affected-minion promotion, shortcut hooks, tests, and docs.

title: Wire browser Sun Cup-then-Disc staging
description:
Browser move selection currently exposes Sun as a distinct power but confirms it to :move-transition-unavailable. Pure game-state/apply-sun-move already supports Cup followed by optional Disc plus created-piece and created-territory shortcuts.

Implement staged browser selection and confirmation for the full Sun sequence while keeping separate Cup and Disc selections available for the card.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Move-selection stages Sun as Cup first, then optional Disc, never Disc before Cup.
- Supports Sun from hand-card and board-territory sources with source cost applied once.
- Supports normal Cup then Disc action targeting for pieces and territories.
- Supports same-created-target shortcuts: create a medium piece without spending a small piece, and create a royalty territory in wasteland without first spending a one-point card.
- Rejections preserve staged selection and do not mutate :game.
- Update app-state tests and browser control option tests for Sun commands.
- Remove the Sun-specific :move-transition-unavailable path once staging is complete.
close_reason: Implemented browser Sun Cup-then-Disc staging

title: Implement deferred Sword-major composite rules
description:
Complete the Sword-related major arcana rules that are currently deferred in README.md, AGENTS.md, and MIND_MAP.md: Justice trades hands before Sword, Death performs Sword twice with same-target shortcut, Tower orients a minion before Sword, and Moon performs Rod then Sword with its full-territory exception.

Single Sword attacks and Tower discard-pile territory replacement already exist; this ticket is for sequencing and special pre/post behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Justice trades hands with the owner of the targeted piece before resolving Sword.
- Death can resolve one or two Sword actions, can split them across minions, and supports same-target shortcut damage without requiring intermediate replacement resources.
- Tower stages and applies minion orientation before validating the Sword target while preserving existing discard-pile replacement behavior.
- Moon stages Rod then Sword in order and allows entering a full territory only when the Sword step leaves no more than three pieces there.
- Hand-source cards are discarded exactly once for each composite major move.
- Board-source activation respects eligible minions for each sub-action.
- Add unit, app-state, and feature coverage for success and rejection cases.
close_reason: done

title: Implement draw, redraw, and discard-pile major powers
description:
Implement major arcana powers centered on deck, hand, and discard-pile manipulation: Fool reveals and optionally plays the next draw-pile card twice, High Priestess performs two discard-and-redraw passes, and Judgement draws selected cards from anywhere in the discard pile up to the minion pip count and hand limit.

Standalone draw/discard-to-six already exists, but these card powers have distinct source, minion, discard, and selection rules.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Fool reveals the top draw-pile card, allows its power to be used or skipped, then repeats once.
- Fool preserves card accounting for revealed cards, hand-source Fool discard, and any generated sub-action source costs.
- High Priestess supports two sequential redraw passes, each discarding any or no hand cards and drawing up to six or fewer.
- Judgement lets the active minion choose cards from anywhere in discard pile up to that minions pip count and the six-card hand limit.
- Hand-source Judgement can draw itself back from discard when legal.
- All paths support deterministic discard-pile reshuffle behavior where relevant.
- Add feature scenarios for optional use, hand limit rejections, and card accounting.
close_reason: Implemented pure Fool, High Priestess, and Judgement draw/discard major powers with tests and docs.

title: Implement Hierophant, Hermit, and Devil powers
description:
Implement the remaining non-suit piece and territory manipulation majors: Hierophant replaces the target piece with one of the current players pieces of the same size and orients it, Hermit moves a targeted piece or territory to an eligible empty space, and Devil orients targeted pieces three times including enemy pieces.

These powers do not map cleanly to the current Cup/Rod/Disc/Sword single-action transition set and need explicit pure transitions plus browser staging.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Hierophant validates target-piece reach, requires same-size current-player stash availability, replaces the target piece, returns the replaced piece to its owner stash, and orients the new piece.
- Hermit can move a targeted piece to any empty territory or wasteland, preserving enemy orientation and allowing own-piece orientation.
- Hermit can move a targeted territory to an eligible wasteland not occupied by enemy pieces, preserving pieces and orientations according to the rule text.
- Devil can orient a target piece three times, including enemy pieces, and recalculates legal targets after orienting the acting minion.
- All transitions preserve stash mirrors, card accounting, board coordinates, and schema invariants.
- Add browser staging and feature scenarios for each power.
close_reason: done

title: Implement remaining composite suit-major powers
description:
Implement the major arcana that compose existing suit actions but need ordered multi-action behavior: Empress orient then unbounded Cup, Emperor orient then unbounded Rod, Lovers Rod then Cup, Chariot Rod twice with same-piece pass-through shortcut, Hanged Man Rod then hand trade, and Temperance Cup twice.

Some individual variants already exist, such as Empress-style Cup and Emperor-style Rod, but their full ordered card text is not yet staged or applied as a single major-card move.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
